records.specific_details: Wrote to the Joint Committee on Justice, Home Affairs and Migration to express urgent concern regarding the challenges faced by persons with disabilities living within the Direct Provision system.
records.intended_results: Request that the Committee considers and discusses the concerns and takes corrective action urgently
lobbying_activities: Collaborative letter co-signed by a collective of advocates, organisations and academics - Letter

lobbying_activities: Aoife McEvilly Coimisiún na Meán cuairt ar TG4 ar 30/5/2025, plé ar mhaoiniú ilbhliaintiúil do TG4 agus ceist Moladh 7.1 do cheannas eagarthóireacht neamhspleách ar Nuacht TG4. Chruinniú le Deirdre Ní Choistín Ard Stiúrthóir. - Meeting

Cruinniú  i TG4 ar 9/7/2025 leis an Ard Stiúrthóir Deirdre Ní Choistín, ard bainistíocht TG4 agus Cathaoirleach TG4  leis an Aire Patrick O'Donovan, Ard Rúnaí Feargal Ó Coigligh agus Rúnaí Cúnta Tríona Quill. Pléadh tosaíochtaí straitéiseacha TG4 do 2026, ach go háirithe maoiniú leordhóthanach agus ilbhliaintiúíl TG4, Neamhspleáchas nuachta agus infheicfheacht agus infhaighteacht ábhair TG4 ar ardáin 3ú páirtí. - Meeting

Cruinniú ar 15/7/2025  faoi thosaíochtaí straitéise TG4 do 2026: i láthair bhí Deirdre Ní Choistín Ard Stiúrthóir TG4, Trevor Ó Clochartaigh TG4, Lís Ní Dhálaigh TG4 agus  Aengus Ó Snodaigh, Joanna Byrne, Conor D. McGuinness agus Shona Ó Raghallaigh - Meeting

records.specific_details: Liaison on the upcoming launch of the Real Face of Men's Health report which makes clear the need for the government to progress the National Men's Health Action plan, with a range of recommendations on priority areas including the need for cross government support statement and clarity on initial funding for the action plan.
records.intended_results: TDs and Ministers show their public support for action on men's health with statements in the Dáil, attendance at events and meetings with Movember and progression of the national men's health action plan as per the recommendation in the Real Face of Men's Health report available here https://ie.movember.com/movember-institute/the-real-face-of-mens-health-report
lobbying_activities: Danny has been contacting TDs and the team for the Minister of Health to arrange meetings ahead of launch and share information on the report - Email
We have met with policy makers on 1 on 1 meetings and via the Movember stand at the National Ploughing Championships 2025 - Meeting

records.specific_details: Special Class Allocation
records.intended_results: Special Class Allocation for children with Down syndrome

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Children, Equality, Disability, Integration and Youth

lobbying_activities: Email follow up to virtual meetings and phone calls, to discuss next steps and outcomes. - Email
Email sent to Maurice Quinlivan TD on Special class allocation for children with Down syndrome. - Email
Email to Cathal Crowe TD- Chairperson of Joint Committee on Education & Youth on allocation of special classes. - Email

Meeting with Minister Moynihan and officials of NCSE, his policy advisor and Fianna Fail TD's, Albert Dolan, John Connolly and Senators Shane Curley and Anne Rabbitte to discuss: criteria for accessing SSLD Classes nationwide and the impact of new criteria; GLD classes for children who have Mild & Moderate learning disabilities; allocation of Special Classes - imbalance in allocation for children who have Down syndrome in comparison to other disabilities. - Meeting
Online meeting with Minister for Special Education- Michael Moynihan, Brendan Doody – Inspectorate DES, Noel Byrne – PA to Minister Moynihan on allocation of special classes. - Virtual Meeting
Online meeting with Shonagh Ni Raghaillaigh, Sinn Fein Spokesperson on Special Education and Brian Mac Donncha, PA to Shonagh Ni Raghaillaigh on allocation of special classes. - Virtual Meeting
Phone call with Alan Kelly TD-Labour Party on allocation of special classes. - Phone call

records.specific_details: Pre-budget submission in relation to rates, funding for Foras na Gaeilge and school libraries and to include bookshops in the culture card scheme.
records.intended_results: To include bookshops in the Culture Card and include booksellers in taskforce to progress its implementation.To increase funding for school libraries and ensure that some funding is ringfenced for spending in local bookshops.to increase funding for Foras na Gaeilge and ensure funding is ringfenced for Áis to fully support Irish language literature. To reform the commercial rates system and commit to monitoring its support of SMEs in town centres.

records.specific_details: Update to members of Oireachtas on 221+ work
records.intended_results: Continued funding for 221+, Commitment & funding to extend services of 221+ to all women let down by the CervicalCheck screening programme, implement the AHR Act and push through Amendments bill, exclude 221+ member court payments from means testing, support gynaecological health, Implement Patient Voice Partner Policy, implement the 2018 Lymphoedema model of care, continued investment in Elimination of Cervical Cancer, Ringfenced funding for women’s health. Event sponsored by Marie Sherlock TD.

lobbying_activities: August 27th: Email to Ireland's 14 MEPs inviting them to an online briefing on the Indecon Report. - Email
July 10th:  The GPA's Pre-Budget Submission was emailed to Jack Chambers TD by GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Submission
July 10th:  The GPA's Pre-Budget Submission was emailed to Patrick O'Donovan TD by GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Submission

July 17th: Meeting with Fianna Fáil representatives facilitates by Paul McAuliffe TD, Fianna Fáil (Chair of Oireachtas Housing Committee) at Leinster House.  Other attendees included John Lahart TD and Cormac Devlin TD.  Other individuals came in and out of the meeting over the course of the hours.  Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley were joined at the meeting by Dublin players Brian Howard, Eve O’Brien, Eoin Murchan and Conor Burke.  Brendan Walsh from Blueyard also attended. - Meeting

July 2nd: Meeting with Seán Canney TD and Padraig Hughes at Leinster House.  This meeting was attended by Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley from the GPA.  they were joined by Galway hurler Conor Whelan and Mandy Johnston from Blueyard. - Meeting
June 11th: Email to Patrick Leonard at the Department of Transport requesting a meeting with Sean Canney TD from GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Email
June 27th: Email to Alan Kelly TD to request an opportunity to present the findings of the Indecon Report to the Oireachtas Committee on Sport.  This email came from GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Email
May 15th: Meeting with Minister Jack Chambers TD.  Tom Parsons attended the meeting on behalf of the GPA at the Department of Finance - Meeting

May 20th: Meeting with Minister Patrick O'Donovan TD, Theresa Newman and Ciaran Shanley at the Department of Sport. Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley attended this meeting on behalf of the GPA and were joined by GPA National Executive Committee member Neil McManus. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Circulation of PSI Press Release to Oireachtas Members (opposition TDs) Calling for an End to the Use of Therapy Notes in Rape and Sexual Assault Cases
records.intended_results: The PSI seeks to secure legislative reform prohibiting the use of therapy notes in legal proceedings involving sexual assault, to protect survivor confidentiality and access to psychological care. PSI aims to establish its expertise as the professional voice for psychology on this issue, influence opposition TDs to support relevant legislation, and prompt Government action on trauma-informed, rights-based policies in response to gender-based violence.
lobbying_activities: PSI circulated a press release calling for an end to the use of therapy notes in sexual assault legal proceedings. The release set out PSI’s rationale against the practice, referenced PSI’s Position Statement, CSO data on sexual violence, ethical guidelines, and survivor concerns. The activity aimed to inform designated public officials, support survivor-centred legislation, and position PSI as a stakeholder in shaping reform. - Email

records.specific_details: Additional funding for Family Resource Centre programme in North Tipperary
records.intended_results: To secure additional funding for the Family Resource Centre programme in North Tipperary

records.specific_details: Briefings providing to Members of the Joint Committee on Media, and Members of the Oireachtas legislation. Meetings with Minister for Minister for Culture, Communications and Sport, and officials, re Budget/Programme for Government funding. Engagement with the Gambling Regulation Authority of Ireland and Coimisiún na Meán and Comreg on mutual priorities. Communications with MEPs and EU Commissioner on EU legislation.
records.intended_results: To have the priorities, concerns and proposals of our organisation's Members considered in hearings, deliberations and report of the Joint Committee and for changes where necessary to be considered.::To urgently progress the access of our sector to funding approved in the last Budget and in the Programme for Government for news and current affairs in independent radio sector.::To seek to have the priorities and concerns of commercial broadcasters in Ireland and Europe considered in relation to EU and Irish legislation on media.::To ensure that IBI was aware of and understood the plans and schedule for the introduction of gambling advertising regulation.::To ensure open and constructive communications with regulatory authorities including strategic plans for the radio sector.::To arrange meetings or ask questions re policy, regulatory or legislative issues.

records.intended_results: To ask if implementation of comprehensive vulnerability assessments, co-ordinated care pathways and designated case workers for those seeking international protection with disabilities will be considered in the workplan.
lobbying_activities: The committee responded to say they acknowledged and noted the email. - Email

records.specific_details: In June 2025, JRS Ireland was a joint signatory to a policy paper submitted to the Committee on Justice, Home Affairs and Migration, containing a series of recommendations regarding the General Scheme of the International  Protection Bill 2025 and Ireland's implementation of the EU Pact on Asylum and Migration.
records.intended_results: Along with other coalition member organisations, JRS Ireland sought to share its analysis of the General Scheme of the International  Protection Bill 2025 and highlight issues of concern for attention by the Committee on Justice, Home Affairs and Migration.
lobbying_activities: Submission
